text:
It appears winning gold at the Paris Olympics is not the only prize contestants are vying for after a Norwegian swimmer turned the humble chocolate muffin into a viral TikTok sensation.

It appears winning gold is not the only prize up for grabs at the Paris Olympics as the humble chocolate muffin has captured the hearts, and stomach’s, of contestants seeking the delectable delight.

Norwegian long-distance swimmer turned dessert connoisseur Henrik Christiansen’s mania for a particular chocolate chunk-encrusted lava filled muffin during his Paris stay has started a TikTok storm.

Christiansen ranked the dessert, baked by French catering company Coup de Pates, an 11/10 on the social media platform and raved about them.

The 27-year-old and said they were not only “insane” but “the single greatest thing about the Olympics so far”.

“They’re very rich, and I like that,” he told the New York Times.

“There is a lot of misconception regarding athletes and what they eat – of course, some athletes, they do sports where their weight is carefully watched and it’s a lot more important.

“On my most heavy training days, I can get up to almost 7000 calories a day. It’s tough to have that many calories if you’re only having salad.

“I’m lucky enough to be able to indulge myself a lot more than maybe other people can because I need the calories that much more.”

Christiansen has dubbed himself the “OLYMPIC MUFFIN MAN” as he surpassed more than 25 million views on his TikTok videos about his love for them.

"I literally cannot stop,” he said.

A trove of Paris competitors also flocked to ranking the sought after desserts highly after Christiansen.

Speaking to People magazine, members of America’s men’s gymnastics team confirmed the chocolate sweets, one of 2000 pastry items served daily at the Olympic Village, were superb.

“I had my first one this morning … oh my gosh, they’re really good. They’re so gooey,” Brody Malone said.

His teammate, Stephen Nedoroscik, added: “It is delicious”.

"I literally cannot stop," he said.

Filipino-American gymnast Aleah Finnegan gave her own review on TikTok: “I understand the hype of these chocolate muffins in the village”.

The 21-year-old in Team Philippines wrote “They are so good like ???” over the clip in which she bit happily into the muffin.

Non-Olympians, although perplexed by the muffin hype, are also vying for a piece of the infamous sweet.

“There’s a Norwegian swimmer who keeps making TikTok’s about how good the chocolate muffins in the Olympic Village cafeteria are and I’ve never wanted something so bad,” one person wrote on X.

“The star of the Olympic village is the chocolate muffin,” said another on Tik Tok.

A baffled third said: “I cannot count how many TikTok’s I've seen about the Olympic chocolate muffin”.

Coup de Pates are also bewildered but thankful for the frenzy over their sweets and the company took to social media to confirm they have a welcomed drum up in business.

“We knew our Maxi Muffin with Intense Chocolate was something special … but we didn't expect it to spark such a chocolate storm!” the company wrote on social media.

“Thank you for all the love.”
